name: Ponte Borela – Río Almofrei trail via Monte Castelo
name_original: Ponte Borela – sendero de Río Almofrei por Monte Castelo
region_intro: Hiking around Cotobade offers diverse natural features, including the Lérez and Almofrei rivers, which provide picturesque riverine paths, natural pools, and waterfalls. The landscape is characterized by mountainous elements such as Mount Seixo and Coto do Castro, an approximately 534-meter hill offering panoramic views. Trails often traverse forests and areas with traditional Galician constructions, providing varied terrain for exploration. The region's hydrological and mountainous features create a rich environment for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cotobade The most popular hiking route is Borela – trail along the Rio Almofrei, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Almofrei River, featuring a gentle elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ponte Borela – Río Almofrei trail via Monte Castelo, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.1 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain and offers expansive views from Monte Castelo. Local hikers also love the Foxo do Lobo desde Carballedo — circular por el río Almofrei, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Cotobade is defined by river valleys, forested areas, and hills, with a network of trails offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cotobade are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 350 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cotobade's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Cotobade?
A: Cotobade offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 95 trails available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging routes, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cotobade?
A: The region is characterized by diverse natural features, including picturesque riverine paths along the Lérez and Almofrei rivers, natural pools, and waterfalls. You'll also encounter mountainous elements like Mount Seixo and Coto do Castro, offering panoramic views, as well as forests and areas with traditional Galician constructions.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in Cotobade?
A: Yes, Cotobade is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The Almofrei River features cascades and natural swimming areas accessible via trails. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6287747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza da Freixa&lt;/a&gt; are significant natural attractions nearby that can be incorporated into your hiking plans.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cotobade?
A: Yes, Cotobade has numerous easy and moderate trails suitable for families. Many routes follow riverbanks, offering gentle terrain and scenic spots like river beaches and recreational areas, such as Playa Fluvial de A Chan - Carballedo, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Cotobade?
A: Many trails in Cotobade are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to respect local regulations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cotobade?
A: Yes, Cotobade offers several excellent circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374591044/foxo-do-lobo-desde-carballedo-circular-por-el-rio-almofrei" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foxo do Lobo desde Carballedo — circular por el río Almofrei&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.8 km loop that takes you through beautiful riverine landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19881050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maneses River loop from A Lagoa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.6 km trail.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks along the trails?
A: Cotobade boasts several viewpoints, such as Mirador de San Xusto and the Area Recreativa del Mirador de A Soldada, which provide stunning vistas. You might also encounter historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3725313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte do Burgo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4411092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Milestone – Km 60&lt;/a&gt;, adding cultural interest to your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cotobade?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Cotobade, with mild temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, but river trails provide opportunities for cooling off. Winter hikes are also possible, though some paths might be wet or muddy.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Cotobade?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988229731/ponte-borela-rio-almofrei-trail-via-monte-castelo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Borela – Río Almofrei trail via Monte Castelo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.1 km path with significant elevation gain, offering expansive views from Monte Castelo. There are 8 difficult routes in the region for those looking for a strenuous adventure.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Cotobade?
A: Many trailheads in Cotobade, especially those near popular river beaches and recreational areas like Playa Fluvial de A Chan - Carballedo or Playa Fluvial y Área Recreativa de Calvelo, offer designated parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before your trip.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest to discover while hiking?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Cotobade's trails often pass through areas rich in history. You can discover ancient bridges, traditional Galician constructions, petroglyphs, and even abandoned villages, offering a glimpse into the region's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Pilgrim Virgin&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical site nearby.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cotobade?
A: The hiking trails in Cotobade are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the river paths, and the well-maintained routes that cater to various skill levels.
tips: Along the Almofrei River over the stone bridge to Pozo Negro, Cotobade, the river's flow is slowed by a stone dam creating a recreational beach with shady trees and public access for cooling off on hot summer days. true secret
Xesteira river beach on the Almofrei river a small dam stops the course of the river on its way to meet the Lerez river creating a small beach to cool off on hot summer days

Borela Bridge, next to the hermitage of Lourdes, in Cotobade. Built over the Almofrei river, this bridge dates back to the 17th century, although we would go back to the 15th century since it was originally a medieval bridge

The hermitage of Lourdes, located a few meters from Ponte Borela, on the Almofrei river, in the parish of Borela. Small rectangular building with a single nave. It is very common in Galicia to build small hermitages next to old bridges, almost forcing travelers to stop and offer prayers and gifts for a blessed journey.

waypoints.name: Xesteira River Beach on the Almofrei River
